brand.title: Delta Constraint Lab
brand.tagline: A 3D storytelling platform for hazardous substructures and river-network constrained urbanization.
brand.heroStatement: Delta growth does not simply spread. It is steered, compressed, and locked into narrow, risky substructures.

tracks.label: Nature Track
tracks.title: Hazardous Substructures
tracks.subtitle: From counterintuitive growth to low-redundancy risk lock-in
tracks.heroSummary: Show how population and built-up growth continue to accumulate in risky delta environments, then reorganize into constrained and low-redundancy substructures under river, levee, and protection effects.

chapters.title: Phenomenon
chapters.summary: Growth does not retreat from hazard. It continues to move into risky, constrained spaces.
chapters.whyItMatters: Editors need the paradox immediately: exposure rises not only because hazards intensify, but because development remains attracted to risky places.
evidence: Population and built-up intensity rise through the timeline instead of retreating from flood-prone areas.
Urban growth clusters around the same narrow corridors that also sustain elevated hazard exposure.

chapters.title: Structure
chapters.summary: Risky growth is not randomly dispersed. It condenses into elongated, river-linked substructures with weak redundancy.
chapters.whyItMatters: The scientific novelty is spatial organization: the problem is not only where risk exists, but what kind of structure risk-producing growth forms.
evidence: Linear belts emerge alongside water and levee-like constraints.
Endpoint-rich growth indicates weak substitute paths and fragile edge conditions.

chapters.title: Mechanism
chapters.summary: River guidance, land constraints, and protection jointly channel development into directional, locked patterns.
chapters.whyItMatters: This section turns a descriptive map into a mechanism narrative that reviewers can test via ablation and sandbox controls.
evidence: Removing river guidance weakens the corridor pattern.
Increasing protection lowers immediate exposure but can intensify long-term lock-in.

chapters.title: Consequence
chapters.summary: Hazard, exposure, and low redundancy overlap, concentrating vulnerability on the most constrained edges.
chapters.whyItMatters: The project must make clear that these structures matter because they carry disproportionate future fragility, not because they merely look linear.
evidence: High-risk cells co-locate with corridor edges and thin settlement chains.
Exposure concentration remains elevated even when overall settlement expansion slows.

chapters.title: Scenario Lab
chapters.summary: The sandbox exposes how five macro drivers reshape hazard substructures without overwhelming the audience with model internals.
chapters.whyItMatters: An editor should be able to play with the mechanism and see a defensible response, not just watch a canned animation.
evidence: Constraint intensity and protection create visible tradeoffs between compactness and lock-in.
Development pressure amplifies exposure fastest when river guidance is already strong.

chapters.title: Figure Studio
chapters.summary: Export a consistent set of publication-grade views, evidence boards, and metric posters.
chapters.whyItMatters: This module turns the site into a figure machine rather than a one-off presentation.
evidence: Six template presets standardize angle, legend, caption, and export ratio.
PNG and SVG exports support both web communication and paper drafting.

tracks.label: Cities Track
tracks.title: RCCU in Pearl River Delta
tracks.subtitle: Prototype objects, water guidance, and emergence dynamics
tracks.heroSummary: Translate the broad hazardous-substructure story into a precise, object-based RCCU narrative in the Pearl River Delta, emphasizing recognizability, interpretation, and editorial clarity.

chapters.title: What Is RCCU
chapters.summary: RCCU is a distinct corridor-like urban phenomenon coupled to waterways and constrained agrarian landscapes.
chapters.whyItMatters: Cities needs a tight definition before any technical detail. Readers must see why RCCU is not ordinary fringe growth.
evidence: Prototype clusters align with narrow water-linked belts rather than compact urban cores.
Core and probable classes form a small, visually interpretable subset instead of exhaustive pixel segmentation.

chapters.title: PRD Timeline
chapters.summary: A multi-step timeline shows how corridor growth thickens, branches, and consolidates across the Pearl River Delta.
chapters.whyItMatters: The timeline is the simplest way to communicate emergence and persistence to both editors and broader audiences.
evidence: Growth begins as thin aligned chains before thickening into composite strips.
The most interpretable objects appear near water and reclaimed agricultural structure.

chapters.title: Prototype Objects
chapters.summary: Representative objects are scored and classified as core, probable, or non-RCCU for explainable interpretation.
chapters.whyItMatters: This is where the system supports article figures and review discussion around identifiable examples.
evidence: Objects with strong water coupling and elongated geometry rise to the top of the ranking.
Urban separation reduces false positives from ordinary compact city expansion.

chapters.title: Water Guidance / Ablation
chapters.summary: Compare vector water guidance, hybrid guidance, and simplified fields to show why water representation matters.
chapters.whyItMatters: The method becomes much more credible when users can see the geometry degrade under weaker guidance assumptions.
evidence: Ablated water guidance broadens the pattern and weakens clear object prototypes.
The hybrid setup preserves river-bank alignment while avoiding overly coarse water masks.

chapters.title: RCCU Sandbox
chapters.summary: A limited control panel allows users to stress-test the emergence of corridor settlements with intuitive macro drivers.
chapters.whyItMatters: The interaction should feel rigorous but still playful enough for external dissemination.
evidence: Higher river guidance increases alignment and object confidence.
Constraint intensity and development pressure jointly shift the balance between plausible and noisy objects.

chapters.title: Figure Studio
chapters.summary: Export editorial figures, object boards, and timeline strips for the Cities manuscript.
chapters.whyItMatters: A polished figure workflow increases the manuscript's portability and visual coherence.
evidence: Template-driven outputs reduce manual layout work.
Object-centric boards directly support prototype presentation in paper and talks.

footer.title: Methods & Data
footer.body: Derived from RCCU simulation exports, risk overlays, and editorial story manifests. The v1 payload is normalized for front-end integration.

footer.title: Metric Definitions
footer.body: Track metrics describe either hazardous substructure dynamics or explainable RCCU object behavior. Each metric is documented in the on-page evidence drawer.

footer.title: About the Model
footer.body: The current platform combines existing simulation outputs with a design-oriented adapter layer so front-end structure is stable before richer data ingestion.
